rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior; use implementation scopes for assign bindings.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Refer to component ports only through valid variables or outputs in type-level contracts.
Declare constants and equations in component type annexes when describing interface behavior.
Use visible identifiers and feature names to define bounds and logical relations in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax includes `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` declarations within `annex agree` blocks.
Component type contracts should define input assumptions and output guarantees using visible feature names and constants.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific assignments in component type scopes.
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE variables unless explicit typed ports or constants are present.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing tokens such as ms/us/Hz/Bytes
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
